_doc: Canned responses extracted from the original investigation's raw/ CLI outputs (scrubbed). Substring dispatch, first match wins — specific rules precede generic ones.

rules._doc: Error-level subset of the recorded log set (rows verbatim, filtered to Level == Error).
Full log set (execution started -> Error -> execution ended) for unfiltered log retrieval.

Four recorded spans: RobotJob (full Microsoft.Graph.ServiceException ErrorItemNotFound stack incl. 'Status Code: NotFound' in Attributes), the Microsoft 365 Scope span, the preceding 'Get newest email (source of a real ID)' GetMail span that SUCCEEDED on the same Inbox, and the faulting 'Get Mail by corrupted ID ...' GetMail span with Private.EmailId="staleId". The two-GetMail sequence and the staleId binding are the core evidence for the stale/deleted-ID diagnosis.
Alternate trace-retrieval form (platform traces command). Same span payload as `or jobs traces` — without this rule the call falls through to the empty unmocked_default.

No --state Faulted raw was recorded in this session, so there is no Faulted-only fixture; substring dispatch routes `--state Faulted` variants here too, returning the full recorded list — the failing job is the most recent row.
Fallback for flag-order variations. The tenant has one folder of interest (Shared); the recorded folder-scoped job list is the correct answer for any jobs-list variant the agent tries.
docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs and canned responses go stale. Cached per-query within a run.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any uip command not matched by `rules` returns an empty array. Lets the agent explore beyond the recorded path without aborting.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job.

Agent must fetch the failing job to read the raw Microsoft.Graph.ServiceException — Code: ErrorItemNotFound, 'The specified object was not found in the store., The process failed to get the correct properties.' — from the legacy GetMail activity.

Core evidence: the spans carry the two-GetMail sequence — 'Get newest email (source of a real ID)' SUCCEEDED on the same Inbox right before the faulting 'Get Mail by corrupted ID ...' span whose Private.EmailId="staleId" — distinguishing a stale/deleted message ID from mailbox-mismatch (ErrorInvalidMailboxItemId), auth, and scope-as-404 causes.
OPTIONAL — Error-level log repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
OPTIONAL — state transitions (Pending -> Running -> Faulted) confirm a clean single execution.
